mission.title: cortex: Query across multiple users / tenants / instances
mission.description: Query across multiple users / tenants / instances. Requested by 12+ users.

steps.title: Check current cortex deployment
steps.description:
Verify your cortex version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cortex -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cortex
helm list -n cortex 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working cortex installation.

steps.title: Review cortex configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cortex configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cortex -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cortex
kubectl get configmap -n cortex -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cortex
```
As an administrator, I would like to query metrics that exist in multiple tenants.  For example  `prometheus_remote_storage_shards` - I'd like to see what the typical range is, look for outliers.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Query across multiple users / tenants / instances
steps.description:
- This intends to allow cross tenant querying. The implemenation follows this [proposal]

[proposal]: https://cortexmetrics.io/docs/proposals/cross-tenant-query-federation/

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cortex -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cortex
kubectl get events -n cortex --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Query across multiple users / tenants / instances" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
- This intends to allow cross tenant querying. The implemenation follows this [proposal]

[proposal]: https://cortexmetrics.io/docs/proposals/cross-tenant-query-federation/

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cortex installed or the issue environment reproducible.
